subject: @tailwind directives
truth: Stop using @tailwind directives; use @import "tailwindcss" instead.
quote: Old: `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;` New: `@import "tailwindcss";`

subject: bg-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using bg-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like bg-black/50 instead.
quote: `bg-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `bg-black/50`.

subject: text-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using text-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like text-black/50 instead.
quote: `text-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `text-black/50`.

subject: border-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using border-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like border-black/50 instead.
quote: `border-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `border-black/50`.

subject: divide-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using divide-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like divide-black/50 instead.
quote: `divide-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `divide-black/50`.

subject: ring-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using ring-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like ring-black/50 instead.
quote: `ring-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `ring-black/50`.

subject: placeholder-opacity-* utilities
truth: Stop using placeholder-opacity-* utilities; use opacity modifiers like placeholder-black/50 instead.
quote: `placeholder-opacity-*` is removed; use opacity modifiers like `placeholder-black/50`.

subject: flex-shrink-* utilities
truth: Stop using flex-shrink-*; use shrink-* instead.
quote: `flex-shrink-*` is renamed to `shrink-*`.

subject: flex-grow-* utilities
truth: Stop using flex-grow-*; use grow-* instead.
quote: `flex-grow-*` is renamed to `grow-*`.

subject: overflow-ellipsis utility
truth: Stop using overflow-ellipsis; use text-ellipsis instead.
quote: `overflow-ellipsis` is renamed to `text-ellipsis`.

subject: decoration-slice utility
truth: Stop using decoration-slice; use box-decoration-slice instead.
quote: `decoration-slice` is renamed to `box-decoration-slice`.

subject: decoration-clone utility
truth: Stop using decoration-clone; use box-decoration-clone instead.
quote: `decoration-clone` is renamed to `box-decoration-clone`.

subject: shadow-sm utility
truth: Stop using shadow-sm; use shadow-xs instead.
quote: `shadow-sm` is renamed to `shadow-xs`.

subject: shadow utility
truth: Stop using shadow (default); use shadow-sm instead.
quote: `shadow` is renamed to `shadow-sm`.

subject: drop-shadow-sm utility
truth: Stop using drop-shadow-sm; use drop-shadow-xs instead.
quote: `drop-shadow-sm` is renamed to `drop-shadow-xs`.

subject: drop-shadow utility
truth: Stop using drop-shadow (default); use drop-shadow-sm instead.
quote: `drop-shadow` is renamed to `drop-shadow-sm`.

subject: blur-sm utility
truth: Stop using blur-sm; use blur-xs instead.
quote: `blur-sm` is renamed to `blur-xs`.

subject: blur utility
truth: Stop using blur (default); use blur-sm instead.
quote: `blur` is renamed to `blur-sm`.

subject: backdrop-blur-sm utility
truth: Stop using backdrop-blur-sm; use backdrop-blur-xs instead.
quote: `backdrop-blur-sm` is renamed to `backdrop-blur-xs`.

subject: backdrop-blur utility
truth: Stop using backdrop-blur (default); use backdrop-blur-sm instead.
quote: `backdrop-blur` is renamed to `backdrop-blur-sm`.

subject: rounded-sm utility
truth: Stop using rounded-sm; use rounded-xs instead.
quote: `rounded-sm` is renamed to `rounded-xs`.

subject: rounded utility
truth: Stop using rounded (default); use rounded-sm instead.
quote: `rounded` is renamed to `rounded-sm`.

subject: outline-none utility
truth: Stop using outline-none; use outline-hidden instead.
quote: `outline-none` is renamed to `outline-hidden`.

subject: ring utility
truth: Stop using ring (default) for the old 3px ring; use ring-3 instead.
quote: `ring` is renamed to `ring-3`.

subject: default ring width
truth: The ring utility now adds a 1px ring instead of 3px; use ring-3 for the old 3px behavior.
quote: Old: the `ring` utility added a `3px` ring. New: the `ring` utility adds a `1px` ring; use `ring-3` for the old behavior.

subject: space-between selector
truth: Space-between utilities now use margin-bottom on :not(:last-child) instead of margin-top on adjacent siblings.
quote: Old CSS: `.space-y-4 > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { margin-top: 1rem; }`. New CSS: `.space-y-4 > :not(:last-child) { margin-bottom: 1rem; }`.

subject: divide selector
truth: Divide utilities now use border-bottom on :not(:last-child) instead of border-top on adjacent siblings.
quote: Old CSS: `.divide-y-4 > :not([hidden]) ~ :not([hidden]) { border-top-width: 4px; }`. New CSS: `.divide-y-4 > :not(:last-child) { border-bottom-width: 4px; }`.

subject: gradient variant behavior
old: overriding part of a gradient with a variant would reset the entire gradient
new: gradient values are preserved; use via-none to unset a three-stop gradient back to a two-stop gradient
truth: Gradient values are now preserved across variants; use via-none to revert a three-stop gradient to two-stop in a specific state.
quote: Old behavior: overriding part of a gradient with a variant would reset the entire gradient. New behavior: gradient values are preserved; use `via-none` to unset a three-stop gradient back to a two-stop gradient in a specific state.

from_fact: overriding part of a gradient with a variant would reset the entire gradient
gradient values are preserved; use via-none to unset a three-stop gradient back to a two-stop gradient

subject: container configuration
old: container utility had configuration options like center and padding
new: use the @utility directive to customize the container utility
truth: Stop configuring container via config options like center/padding; use the @utility directive to customize container instead.
quote: Old: the `container` utility had configuration options like `center` and `padding`. New: use the `@utility` directive to customize the `container` utility.

from_fact: container utility had configuration options like center and padding
use the @utility directive to customize the container utility

subject: default border color
old: border-* and divide-* utilities used gray-200 by default
new: border-* and divide-* utilities use currentColor
truth: Border and divide utilities now default to currentColor instead of gray-200; specify a color explicitly.
quote: Old: `border-*` and `divide-*` utilities used your configured `gray-200` color by default. New: `border-*` and `divide-*` utilities use `currentColor`. Specify a color anywhere you are using a `border-*` or `divide-*` utility.

from_fact: border-* and divide-* utilities used gray-200 by default
border-* and divide-* utilities use currentcolor

subject: default ring color
old: ring utility default color was blue-500
new: ring utility default color is currentColor
truth: The ring utility now defaults to currentColor instead of blue-500; add ring-blue-500 where you depended on the old default.
quote: Old: the `ring` utility default color was `blue-500`. New: the `ring` utility default color is `currentColor`. Add `ring-blue-500` anywhere you were depending on the default ring color.

from_fact: ring utility default color was blue-500
ring utility default color is currentcolor

subject: default placeholder color
old: placeholder text used gray-400 by default
new: placeholder text uses current text color at 50% opacity
truth: Placeholder text now uses current text color at 50% opacity instead of gray-400.
quote: Old: placeholder text used your configured `gray-400` color by default. New: placeholder text uses the current text color at 50% opacity.

from_fact: placeholder text used gray-400 by default
placeholder text uses current text color at 50% opacity

subject: button cursor
truth: Buttons now use cursor: default instead of cursor: pointer to match browser default behavior.
quote: Old: buttons used `cursor: pointer`. New: buttons use `cursor: default` to match the default browser behavior.

subject: dialog margins
old: Preflight did not reset margins on <dialog> elements
new: Preflight now resets margins on <dialog> elements
truth: Preflight now resets margins on <dialog> elements, which may cause layout shifts.
quote: Old: Preflight did not reset margins on `<dialog>` elements. New: Preflight now resets margins on `<dialog>` elements.

from_fact: preflight did not reset margins on <dialog> elements
preflight now resets margins on <dialog> elements

subject: hidden attribute vs display classes
old: display classes like block or flex took priority over the hidden attribute
truth: Stop relying on display classes to override the hidden attribute; remove the hidden attribute to make an element visible.
quote: Old: display classes like `block` or `flex` took priority over the `hidden` attribute. New: the `hidden` attribute takes priority; remove the `hidden` attribute if you want an element to be visible.

from_fact: display classes like block or flex took priority over the hidden attribute

subject: prefix syntax
old: prefixes did not look like variants
new: prefixes now look like variants and are always at the beginning of the class name, e.g. tw:flex tw:bg-red-500 tw:hover:bg-red-600
truth: Stop using the old prefix format; write prefixes as variant-style prefixes at the start of the class name (e.g. tw:flex).
quote: Old: prefixes did not look like variants. New: prefixes now look like variants and are always at the beginning of the class name, e.g. `tw:flex tw:bg-red-500 tw:hover:bg-red-600`.

from_fact: prefixes did not look like variants
prefixes now look like variants and are always at the beginning of the class name, e.g. tw:flex tw:bg-red-500 tw:hover:bg-red-600

subject: important modifier placement
old: ! was placed at the beginning of the utility name after variants: flex! bg-red-500!
new: ! is placed at the very end of the class name: flex bg-red-500 hover:bg-red-600/50!
truth: Stop putting ! after the utility name; put ! at the very end of the entire class name.
quote: Old: `!` was placed at the beginning of the utility name after variants: `flex! bg-red-500!`. New: `!` is placed at the very end of the class name: `flex bg-red-500 hover:bg-red-600/50!`.

from_fact: ! was placed at the beginning of the utility name after variants: flex! bg-red-500!
! is placed at the very end of the class name: flex bg-red-500 hover:bg-red-600/50!

subject: custom utilities syntax
truth: Stop using @layer utilities with regular CSS; use the new @utility directive instead.
quote: Old: `@layer utilities { .tab-4 { tab-size: 4; } }`. New: `@utility tab-4 { tab-size: 4; }`.

subject: variant stacking order
old: stacked variants applied from right to left: first:*:pt-0 last:*:pb-0
new: stacked variants apply left to right: *:first:pt-0 *:last:pb-0
truth: Stop writing stacked variants in right-to-left order; write them left-to-right so the first variant applies first.
quote: Old: stacked variants applied from right to left: `first:*:pt-0 last:*:pb-0`. New: stacked variants apply left to right: `*:first:pt-0 *:last:pb-0`.

from_fact: stacked variants applied from right to left: first:*:pt-0 last:*:pb-0
stacked variants apply left to right: *:first:pt-0 *:last:pb-0

subject: variables in arbitrary values syntax
truth: Stop using square brackets with CSS variables in arbitrary values; use parentheses instead.
quote: Old syntax: `bg-[--brand-color]`. New syntax: `bg-(--brand-color)`.

subject: commas in arbitrary values for grid and object-position
old: commas were used in grid-cols-*, grid-rows-*, and object-* utilities: grid-cols-[max-content,auto]
new: underscores must be used to represent spaces: grid-cols-[max-content_auto]
truth: Stop using commas to separate values in grid-cols-*, grid-rows-*, and object-* arbitrary values; use underscores instead.
quote: Old: commas were used in `grid-cols-*`, `grid-rows-*`, and `object-*` utilities: `grid-cols-[max-content,auto]`. New: underscores must be used to represent spaces: `grid-cols-[max-content_auto]`.

from_fact: commas were used in grid-cols-*, grid-rows-*, and object-* utilities: grid-cols-[max-content,auto]
underscores must be used to represent spaces: grid-cols-[max-content_auto]

subject: hover variant on mobile
old: the hover variant applied on all devices
new: the hover variant only applies when the primary input device supports hover: @media (hover: hover)
truth: Stop relying on hover styles working on touch devices; hover now only applies when the device supports hover.
quote: Old: the `hover` variant applied on all devices. New: the `hover` variant only applies when the primary input device supports hover: `@media (hover: hover)`.

from_fact: the hover variant applied on all devices
the hover variant only applies when the primary input device supports hover: @media (hover: hover)

subject: transition and transition-colors including outline-color
old: transition and transition-colors utilities did not include outline-color
new: transition and transition-colors utilities now include the outline-color property
truth: Be aware that transition and transition-colors now animate outline-color, which may cause unintended transitions.
quote: Old: `transition` and `transition-colors` utilities did not include `outline-color`. New: `transition` and `transition-colors` utilities now include the `outline-color` property.

from_fact: transition and transition-colors utilities did not include outline-color
transition and transition-colors utilities now include the outline-color property

subject: rotate, scale, translate properties
old: rotate-*, scale-*, and translate-* were based on the transform property
new: they are based on individual rotate, scale, and translate CSS properties
truth: Stop relying on rotate-*, scale-*, and translate-* using the CSS transform property; they now use individual CSS properties.
quote: Old: `rotate-*`, `scale-*`, and `translate-*` were based on the `transform` property. New: they are based on individual `rotate`, `scale`, and `translate` CSS properties.

from_fact: rotate-*, scale-*, and translate-* were based on the transform property
they are based on individual rotate, scale, and translate css properties

subject: resetting transforms
new: reset individual properties instead: scale-150 focus:scale-none
truth: Stop using transform-none to reset transforms; use the individual property's -none variant instead (e.g. scale-none).
quote: Old: reset via `transform-none`: `scale-150 focus:transform-none`. New: reset individual properties instead: `scale-150 focus:scale-none`.

from_fact: reset individual properties instead: scale-150 focus:scale-none

subject: transition with individual transform properties
truth: Stop using 'transform' in transition arbitrary values when animating scale/rotate/translate; use the individual property name instead.
quote: Old: `transition-[opacity,transform] hover:scale-150`. New: `transition-[opacity,scale] hover:scale-150`.

subject: corePlugins option
old: the corePlugins option was available to disable certain utilities
truth: Stop using the corePlugins option; it is no longer supported in v4.
quote: Old: the `corePlugins` option was available to disable certain utilities. New: the `corePlugins` option is no longer supported in v4.

from_fact: the coreplugins option was available to disable certain utilities

subject: theme() function syntax
new: use CSS variables var(--color-red-500) and theme(--breakpoint-xl)
truth: Stop using dot notation in the theme() function; use CSS variables or the new theme(--breakpoint-xl) syntax.
quote: Old: dot notation `theme(colors.red.500)` and `theme(screens.xl)`. New: use CSS variables `var(--color-red-500)` and `theme(--breakpoint-xl)`.

from_fact: use css variables var(--color-red-500) and theme(--breakpoint-xl)

subject: JavaScript config file loading
old: JavaScript config files were detected automatically
new: JavaScript config files must be loaded explicitly using the @config directive
truth: Stop relying on automatic detection of JS config files; use @config "../../tailwind.config.js"; to load them explicitly.
quote: Old: JavaScript config files were detected automatically. New: JavaScript config files must be loaded explicitly using the `@config` directive: `@config "../../tailwind.config.js";`.

from_fact: javascript config files were detected automatically
javascript config files must be loaded explicitly using the @config directive

subject: corePlugins, safelist, separator from JS config
old: corePlugins, safelist, and separator options from JavaScript config were supported
truth: Stop using corePlugins, safelist, and separator in JavaScript config; these options are not supported in v4.0.
quote: The `corePlugins`, `safelist`, and `separator` options from JavaScript config are not supported in v4.0.

from_fact: coreplugins, safelist, and separator options from javascript config were supported

subject: resolveConfig function
old: the resolveConfig function was exported to turn JavaScript config into a flat object
truth: Stop using resolveConfig; it has been removed. Use CSS variables directly or getComputedStyle to access theme values.
quote: Old: the `resolveConfig` function was exported to turn JavaScript config into a flat object. New: the function is removed; use CSS variables directly or `getComputedStyle` to access theme values.

from_fact: the resolveconfig function was exported to turn javascript config into a flat object

subject: @apply in Vue, Svelte, or CSS Modules
old: stylesheets bundled separately had access to theme variables and custom utilities
new: they do not; use @reference or CSS theme variables directly instead of @apply
truth: Stop using @apply in separately-bundled stylesheets (Vue, Svelte, CSS Modules); use @reference or CSS variables directly.
quote: Old: stylesheets bundled separately had access to theme variables and custom utilities. New: they do not; use `@reference "../../app.css";` or use CSS theme variables directly instead of `@apply`.

from_fact: stylesheets bundled separately had access to theme variables and custom utilities
they do not; use @reference or css theme variables directly instead of @apply

subject: browser support requirements
new: Tailwind CSS v4.0 is designed for Safari 16.4+, Chrome 111+, and Firefox 128+
truth: Tailwind v4 no longer supports older browsers; it requires Safari 16.4+, Chrome 111+, or Firefox 128+.
quote: Old: supported older browsers. New: Tailwind CSS v4.0 is designed for Safari 16.4+, Chrome 111+, and Firefox 128+.

from_fact: tailwind css v4.0 is designed for safari 16.4+, chrome 111+, and firefox 128+

subject: PostCSS plugin package
truth: Stop using the tailwindcss package as a PostCSS plugin; use the dedicated @tailwindcss/postcss package instead.
quote: Old: the `tailwindcss` package was a PostCSS plugin. New: the PostCSS plugin lives in a dedicated `@tailwindcss/postcss` package.

subject: Tailwind CLI command
truth: Stop running npx tailwindcss; use npx @tailwindcss/cli instead.
quote: Old: `npx tailwindcss -i input.css -o output.css`. New: `npx @tailwindcss/cli -i input.css -o output.css`.

subject: CSS preprocessor support (Sass, Less, Stylus)
old: Tailwind CSS v3 worked with Sass, Less, and Stylus
truth: Stop using Tailwind CSS v4.0 with CSS preprocessors like Sass, Less, or Stylus; it is not designed to work with them.
quote: Old: could be used with Tailwind CSS v3. New: Tailwind CSS v4.0 is not designed to be used with CSS preprocessors like Sass, Less, or Stylus.

from_fact: tailwind css v3 worked with sass, less, and stylus
